author: @maranmarantakeiteazie
text: very true...when you take the first step and realise you can do something, confidence and ideas grow.  i also take the point about 'fear' and the danger it has in stopping people from doing, especially when other people use it to supress people's natural desire to grow.....I always remind myself that there are no mistakes, only growth when doing.

author: @ibme8359
text: Don't like the title "Dreaming And Doing"!!! How about "Desire and Accomplishment"!!! You sir are not a dreamer, lets try "INNOVATOR"!!!

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: Happy New year ! The point you make is absolutely right , but I think that your intro is a bit strange. Teenagers can be rebellious and can behave in a strange ways , but , small children screaming , because they didn't get an ice cream is not a thing you see often , where I live (it happens occasionally). When I was a small boy , I was allowed to ask for something , but no meant no and screaming (in public , or otherwise) would have very hurtful and far reaching consequences . It was not because my parents couldn't afford it (whatever it was) , but because they tried to teach us , that we can't have everything we want and/or like and to value things we have. We were , also ,thought to share things , so that other kids wouldn't feel like outsiders. Different social arrangements , I guess....

author: @frankfaubert1927
text:
Great video Rob. 

It seems that we've been trained to visualize all possible outcomes before we start so as not to waste our employer's resources.

I like your way better.
